skill.name: cohere-v2-python
skill.description: Guide for using Cohere v2 Chat API with Python, focused on JSON Schema mode, structured outputs, and entity extraction workflows.
skill.summary: Build Cohere Python workflows for structured entity extraction and validated JSON outputs.
skill.author: ActiveInferenceInstitute

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, and credential patterns, but review found Markdown documentation examples rather than executable skill code. No prompt injection attempt or malicious exfiltration logic was found; remaining risk is low because users are guided to use Cohere APIs and API keys.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported backtick and command patterns occur inside Markdown code fences and inline API documentation, not executable skill scripts. The examples include package installation and Python snippets that a user may choose to run manually.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain documentation and fenced code examples. I found no executable wrapper, hook, or automation that runs these commands on behalf of the skill.

low_findings.title: API Key Handling Appears Legitimate But Requires Care
low_findings.description: Verdict: NEEDS_REVIEW_LOW. The skill shows placeholder Cohere API key usage and environment-variable configuration. This is expected for Cohere integration, but users should avoid committing real keys or sending sensitive text without review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The key values are placeholders or environment-variable references. The risk is operational secret handling, not evidence of embedded credential theft.

low_findings.title: External Network Use Is Intended API Documentation
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE_LOW. The skill teaches use of Cohere services and links to Cohere, PyPI, GitHub, and JSON Schema resources. This is expected for an API skill, but it means extracted data may leave the local environment when examples are implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced network destinations are normal documentation and package locations. I found no hidden endpoint or unauthorized background request.

low_findings.title: Prompt Override Language Is API Guidance, Not Injection
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The skill discusses system-message priority as part of Cohere API behavior. It does not instruct the auditing agent or marketplace user to ignore security instructions or change review outcomes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious wording appears in developer guidance for model prompting. I found no text claiming authority over this audit or asking reviewers to skip analysis.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable structured data from unstructured text. This skill shows how to use Cohere v2, Python, and JSON Schema mode for validated extraction workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains Cohere ClientV2 setup for Python chat requests.
Shows JSON Schema mode for validated structured outputs.
Provides entity extraction patterns for people, organizations, dates, and documents.
Covers enum-based classification for constrained labels.
Documents streaming, RAG, tool use, parameters, and error handling references.
Lists schema limitations and production considerations.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not provide runnable package code.
Examples require a valid Cohere API key and network access.
Model availability and pricing may change outside this skill.
Structured output quality still depends on prompt design and source text quality.

use_cases.title: Extract customer records from messages
use_cases.description: Convert support notes into names, organizations, dates, and issue categories using a validated response schema.

use_cases.title: Prototype an NER pipeline
use_cases.description: Design schema-first entity extraction flows before integrating results into downstream databases or review queues.

use_cases.title: Validate document parsing outputs
use_cases.description: Create structured extraction prompts for resumes, invoices, business cards, and medical-style reports.

prompt_templates.title: Start a basic extraction
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for one text sample.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the cohere-v2-python skill to create a simple Python example that extracts person, organization, location, and date fields from one paragraph.

prompt_templates.title: Design a schema for documents
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the cohere-v2-python skill to design a JSON Schema mode extraction plan for invoices with vendor, line items, dates, taxes, and totals.

prompt_templates.title: Add validation and errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the cohere-v2-python skill to improve my Cohere extraction workflow with parsing checks, required fields, retries, and rate-limit handling.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a batch extraction pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the cohere-v2-python skill to architect a batch entity extraction pipeline with schema reuse, model settings, token monitoring, and database storage.

output_examples.input: A user asks for an entity extraction example for a conference announcement.
output_examples.output: A concise Python-oriented plan with required fields, a Cohere chat request shape, and validation notes.

output_examples.input: A developer asks how to classify product feedback with fixed labels.
output_examples.output: A schema design that uses enum values for sentiment and aspect labels, plus prompt guidance for stable outputs.

output_examples.input: A data team asks whether JSON Schema mode supports their constraints.
output_examples.output: A limitation review covering unsupported numeric ranges, array lengths, string lengths, and complex regex behavior.

best_practices: Start with the smallest useful schema, then add optional fields after validation succeeds.
Use enums for categories that feed automation or database logic.
Keep Cohere API keys in environment variables or secret managers.
anti_patterns: Do not paste production secrets into prompts, examples, or source control.
Do not send regulated or private text to external APIs without approval.
Do not assume schema enforcement replaces business validation.

faq.question: Does this skill include executable code?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation skill with Python examples and implementation guidance.

faq.question: Which Cohere API does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Cohere v2 Chat API usage through the Python client.

faq.question: Can it help with named entity recognition?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes NER-style schemas for people, organizations, locations, dates, events, and products.

faq.question: Does it require a Cohere API key?
faq.answer: Yes. Running the examples requires a valid Cohere API key and network access.

faq.question: Does JSON Schema mode support every JSON Schema feature?
faq.answer: No. The skill notes unsupported constraints such as numeric ranges, array lengths, and string lengths.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production pipelines?
faq.answer: It provides production guidance, but teams still need security review, monitoring, validation, and error handling.
